title: Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica
what_description: Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica is a plant species within the family Rosaceae. It is characterized by its glabrous stems, 10-40 cm in length, with leaves that are pinnately compound, having 3-9 leaflets. Each leaflet is 2-7 cm in length, lanceolate to elliptic in shape, with serrate margins. The flowers are small, 5-6 mm in diameter, with 5 white petals and a yellow center, arranged in corymbose inflorescences. The fruit is a pome, 4-5 mm in diameter, containing 2-3 seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are inflammation, characterized by reduced swelling and pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties. Diarrhea and gastroenteritis can be treated with extracts from this plant, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, fever and osteoarthritis have been effectively managed with compounds isolated from Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica, which have demonstrated analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. Further studies have also revealed that it may help manage diabetes, digestive issues, hypertension, skin infection, gastritis, wound healing, dyspepsia, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as gout. Its antioxidant effects have been linked to the prevention of cancer and skin irritation, as well as the alleviation of asthma symptoms and cough.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, offering protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. This plant also exhibits antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi, thanks to the action of saponins and terpenoids. The anti-inflammatory properties of Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are mediated by flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which modulate the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Additionally, the plant has been shown to possess analgesic properties, alleviating pain through the action of alkaloids and phenolic compounds that interact with opioid receptors. Furthermore, the antifungal activity of Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica is attributed to the presence of terpenoids and phenolic acids, which disrupt fungal cell membranes and inhibit their growth.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are flavonoids and phenolic acids which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Studies have identified quercetin and kaempferol as major flavonoids present in this plant, exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Additionally, the presence of tannins contributes to the plant's astringent properties, which can aid in wound healing and infection control. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica also possess antimicrobial and antitumor properties, making it a potential candidate for further research in the development of new medicines. The combination of these bioactive compounds highlights the plant's potential for the treatment of various diseases.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are infusion, tea, tincture, salve, and decoction. Infusion is made by steeping dried flowers in boiling water, used to treat gastrointestinal disorders. Decoction is prepared by simmering root and stem in water, applied topically for skin issues. Tincture is a concentrated liquid extract, traditionally used to treat respiratory problems. A salve is prepared by infusing the plant in a carrier oil, applied topically for wound healing and skin irritation.
side_effects_description: The possible side effect of using Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica are nausea and diarrhea due to the presence of toxic compounds such as glycosides and alkaloids that can cause gastrointestinal irritation. These compounds can also lead to dizziness and disorientation in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can result in more severe side effects such as kidney damage due to the accumulation of toxic substances in the body. This is because the plant's toxic compounds can be nephrotoxic, meaning they can harm the kidneys and disrupt their normal functioning. Therefore, it is essential to handle and consume Drymocallis longisepala subsp. epirotica with caution and under the guidance of a medical professional or an experienced practitioner.
